paper_title: Model of sediment transport and infiltration

methods.description: This method models the transport and infiltration of fine-grained sediment inland due to storm surges, particularly from hurricanes and tropical storms. It explores the mechanisms by which marine mud is mobilized and deposited inland, and how it infiltrates into Pleistocene limestone.

steps.description: Identify storm surge as the mechanism for onshore transport of fine-grained sediment.
steps.searchable_summary: model storm surge effects on sediment transport

steps.description: Analyze historical data on hurricane frequency and strength to estimate potential sediment transport events.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze historical hurricane data for sediment transport potential

steps.description: Develop a model for deposition of aragonitic mud in large pores of subaerially exposed Pleistocene limestone through storm surge deposition.
steps.searchable_summary: model aragonitic mud deposition in limestone pores

steps.description: Simulate the infiltration of storm-deposited mud into limestone by gravity and percolating rainfall.

methods.description: This method investigates the mineralogical composition of sediment infilling Pleistocene rock, focusing on the absence of high-Mg calcite and the presence of aragonite and low-Mg calcite.

steps.description: Analyze sediment mineralogy from cores to determine the composition of high-Mg calcite, aragonite, and low-Mg calcite.
Model potential mechanisms for the absence of high-Mg calcite, including selective source, sorting, and diagenesis.
steps.searchable_summary: model mechanisms for high-Mg calcite absence

steps.description: Propose further sediment analysis to test the hypothesis of selective dissolution of high-Mg calcite.
steps.searchable_summary: propose further analysis for selective dissolution hypothesis
